characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: Color-Coded Characters: Purple/pink for Amethyst, red for Ruby, blue for Sapphire, yellow/gold for Topaz, and black/dark purple for Onyx. Curtains Match the Window: Their hair and eye colors are similar, with the exception of Amethyst whose purple eyes don't match her pink hair. Five Temperament Ensemble: TheNice GirlAmethyst is Phlegmatic, theFiery RedheadRuby is Choleric, the studious and reserved Sapphire is Supine, theGothDeadpan SnarkerOnyx is Melancholic, and the bubblyCloudcuckoolanderTopaz is Sanguine. Rock Theme Naming: All are named after various precious gemstones, of which they take their theme colors from. True Companions: They're a group of best friends.

characters.name: As a Group
characters.description: Color-Coded Characters: Purple/pink for Amethyst, red for Ruby, blue for Sapphire, yellow/gold for Topaz, and black/dark purple for Onyx. Curtains Match the Window: Their hair and eye colors are similar, with the exception of Amethyst whose purple eyes don't match her pink hair. Five Temperament Ensemble: TheNice GirlAmethyst is Phlegmatic, theFiery RedheadRuby is Choleric, the studious and reserved Sapphire is Supine, theGothDeadpan SnarkerOnyx is Melancholic, and the bubblyCloudcuckoolanderTopaz is Sanguine. Rock Theme Naming: All are named after various precious gemstones, of which they take their theme colors from. True Companions: They're a group of best friends.

characters.name: Amethyst
characters.description: The leader of the BFFL. She is a total sweetheart, and acts as the heart of the BFFL. She's generally kind and eager-to-please, but can be hard on herself in tough situations. Amethyst was the last of her friends to get her gem glow, and finds she's a natural spellcaster. Her boyfriend is the cute, clumsy Coal, and her Ancient is her grandma. Affectionate Nickname: Her grandmother calls her "Ammy". Audience Surrogate: For the first few episodes, the audience gets to know the setting from Amethyst's POV. Badass Adorable: All of the BFFL is this, but her sweet personality makes her stand out. Beware the Nice Ones: She's a sweetheart for sure, but she's just as strong as her friends when things get hairy.

characters.name: Ruby
characters.description: Ruby Trollman is the second-in-command the BFFL. Coming from a rich family, she can be spoiled, self-centered, and abrasive, and has trouble asking for help or admitting when she makes mistakes. Ruby's also not the best spellcaster, but she has nerves of steel and never backs down from a challenge. Her boyfriend is the muscle-bound Rock, and her Ancient is Obsidian. Badass Adorable: She storms Simon's lair and saves her friends with Coal and Rock's help. Belligerent Sexual Tension: With Rock. They bicker as much as they flirt, if not more so. Curtains Match the Window: Invoked in her case, as her red hair is dyed to match her eyes. Her real hair color is brown. Cute Witch: Not the best at spellcasting though…

characters.name: Sapphire
characters.description: Sapphire Trollzawa is smart and ambitious, and takes studying and learning more seriously than her other friends. She can be shy at times, but Sapphire knows quite a lot about history, science, and much more, making her useful in dangerous situations. Her boyfriend is the nerdy Alabaster, and her Ancient is Mr. Trollheimer. Amicable Exes: She dated Rock Trollhammer (Ruby's current boyfriend) sometime prior to the start of the series, but broke up with him for unknown reasons—however, they're on good terms to the point of where Sapphire clearly has no problem with Rock dating Ruby. Asian and Nerdy: She's a smart girl of Japanese descent. As Long as It Sounds Foreign: The suffix "zawa" is indeed a real suffix in Japanese surnames (ex. "Takizawa", "Hanazawa"). However, the letter "L" is not present in the Japanese alphabet, and there is always a vowel in between each consonant, therefore her name would be more likeTororuzawa(トロルザワ). Badass Adorable: She's a smart and kind of shy girl who uses her smarts to help her friends out of tough situations.

characters.name: Onyx
characters.description: Onyx von Trollenberg is a not-so-scary goth. She has morbid interests and can be sarcastic at times, but she's a genuinely good friend who's willing to listen, even if she strikes off alone at times or has trouble asking others for help. Onyx is not good at driving, but is good at casting spells. Her boyfriend is the emo poet Flint, and her Ancient is Spinell. Cannot Tell a Lie: She's very honest. Cute Witch: Her morbidity and snarkdownplaysthe "cute" part at times, but she's great with spellcasting. Dark Is Not Evil: She's got dark purple hair and dark clothes but she's one of the heroes. Deadpan Snarker: She has the sharpest wit of theBFFLs.

characters.name: Topaz
characters.description: Topaz Trollhopper is a fashionista, and isn't always aware of what's going on at times. Silly, sweet, and serious when she has to be, Topaz tends towards the quirky side of things, which can help her friends in a bind. Her boyfriend is the hip-hop cool guy Jasper, and her Ancient is Zirconia. Alliterative Name: Both her first name and her surname begin with "T". Badass Adorable: She's a sweet and quirky girl whose quirky ideas can actually help her friends out of sticky situations. Beware the Silly Ones: She's quirky most of the time, but when things get serious, she can be too. Cloudcuckoolander: Her brain can go to some weird places sometimes.

characters.name: Obsidian
characters.description: The operator of the spell shop at the Trollzopolis Mall, Obsidian has a very wide array of knowledge. She and the other Ancients fought Simon 3,000 years ago. She is the Ancient counterpart to Ruby because of both always taking command. Cool Old Lady: She listen and gives good advice to Ruby about her friends turning into jerks. Mentor Archetype: Though she mentors all five of the BFFL as a whole and was the first of the five Ancients that the girls met, Obsidian's definitely the closest to Ruby Trollman, especially since Obsidian is Ruby's Ancient. Never Mess with Granny: She's one of the Ancients and can still be powerful when she needs to be.

characters.name: Grandma van der Troll
characters.description: One of the five Ancients who is not only Amethyst's grandmother, but is also her granddaughter's Ancient. Similar to Obsidian, while Grandma van der Troll is obviously closer to Amethyst than the other four members of BFFL, she mentors all five of them as a whole and is always eager to listen or help if the situation calls for it. She is knowledgeable about spells, and has several magic artifacts in her possession for safekeeping. Her granddaughter is her counterpart, andThe Heartof the original Ancients. Cool Old Lady: She's helpful and a good listener, and pretty powerful too. Never Mess with Granny: She's quite knowledgeable about spells and has many magic artifacts for safekeeping. No Name Given: Her name is never revealed.

characters.name: Mr. Trollheimer
characters.description: The girls' science teacher at Trollzopolis High School who's one of the five Ancients who fought Simon 3,000-years ago and is eventually revealed to be the Ancient-counterpart of Sapphire,The Smart Guyof the BFFL. Butt-Monkey: He tends to get injured/captured a lot and often has other embarrassing things happen to him. Cool Old Guy: He's an Ancient, this is kind of their job description. Cool Teacher: He's the girls' science teacher and a pretty alright guy. Hot Teacher: Sapphire commented that she finds Mr. Trollheimer attractive.

characters.name: Zirconia
characters.description: Topaz's Ancient who also happens to be Spinell's wife. She fought Simon long ago alongside the other Ancients, but it came at a cost—he imprisoned her within a tree for 3,000 years, draining her magic. At first, Topaz was in denial of Zirconia being her Ancient, but eventually came to accept it, especially when she and Zirconia bonded over a common interest in fashion. Much like Topaz, Zirconia can be rather quirky, but she's also a powerful sorceress and has psychic powers. And I Must Scream:Spent 3,000 years as a tree, while conscious the entire time. Cloudcuckoolander: Like Topaz, she's something of an oddball. Cool Old Lady: She's quite quirky though. Forced Transformation:Was transformed into a tree.

characters.name: Spinell
characters.description: Onyx's Ancient who also happens to Zirconia's husband. Much like his wife, Simon imprisoned him for 3,000 years—this time as a fire-breathing dragon made to guard the tree Zirconia became. Spinell tends to be snappy and sarcastic, but is good at magic, even if the long imprisonment drained his powers some. And just like his wife, Spinell has psychic powers. And I Must Scream:Spent 3,000 years as a dragon who couldn't talk. Cool Old Guy: Though more snarky than the other Ancients. Cursed with Awesome:Was transformed into a dragon for years. While he couldn't talk, it's certainly better than what happened to his wife. Happily Married: ToZirconia.

characters.name: Simon
characters.description: The main villain of the series. Simon is a gremlin who's over 3,000 years old, and was banished to the Shadow World for imprisoning two of the five Ancients, turning most of the good magic evil, and destroying the world of the Trollz as they knew it. His actions led to magic being greatly reduced for Trollz as a whole. Once the BFFL accidentally free him, he intends to finish what he began and conquer the world—but luckily, the Magic of the Five can put an end to his evil plans. Simon isn't one to give up so easily, though, and returns again and again in his attempts to take over Trollzopolis. Big Bad: He's the series' main villain. Disguised in Drag: In one episode, he disguised himself as a girl troll named Garnet. Evil Is Petty: Most of his schemes are motivated by pettiness. Evil Redhead: He's ginger and is evil.

characters.name: Snarf
characters.description: Simon's sidekick and pet. Snarf looks like a cute puppy, but can transforming into a ravenous, rampaging ogre-beast at will. Luckily, Snarf isn't too bright, and he tends to be banished alongside his master whenever their plans are foiled. Butt-Monkey: Simon tends to belittle him a lot. Cute Is Evil: He looks like an adorable puppy, but he's actually a monster as evil as his master. The Dragon: He's Simon's main minion. Non-Human Humanoid Hybrid: Snarf is said to be half ogre and half dog. Which leads tosquickabout how he came about, he did mention having a mother once...

characters.name: Coal
characters.description: Amethyst's boyfriend. Coal is cute and clumsy, and is generally the most sensible of the boys—but he does have a mischievous side now and then. He rides a hoverboard. Distaff Counterpart: He's mild-mannered like Amethyst. The Klutz: The first time we see him he trips over a garbage can and drops a surfboard on his foot. Nice Guy: He's a sweetheart, like his girlfriend. Not So Above It All: When the boys start pelting each other with sacred amber, he tries to stop it. Then he gets hit with amber himself he joins in on the fight.

characters.name: Rock
characters.description: Ruby's boyfriend. He likes to play in sporting events such as Trollball. Amicable Exes: He and Sapphire dated sometime prior to the start of the series, but she broke up with him for unknown reasons—but they appear to be on good terms and she seems perfectly fine with him going out with Ruby. Belligerent Sexual Tension: With Ruby. They bicker as much as they flirt, if not more so. Big Eater: According to his character bio, he can eat 23 Trollburgers in one sitting. Distaff Counterpart: He's a single-minded fighter like Ruby.

characters.name: Alabaster
characters.description: Sapphire's boyfriend. While nerdy, Alabaster comes from a rich family, and his state-of-the-art technology has helped the BFFL more than once. Badass Bookworm: He uses his tech smarts to save Coal and Flint from falling into a chasm. Bully Magnet: He gets picked on by the rest of the boys for being a nerd. Cannot Spit It Out: He's in love with Sapphire, but he's too shy to tell her how he really feels. The Comically Serious: He doesn't have much of a sense of humor, which makes him aBully Magnetfor the other boys.

characters.name: Jasper
characters.description: Topaz's boyfriend. He used to have hair until a hair-removal spell was cast, but dug how it went with his hip-hop style. Jasper likes parties and music, as well as being cool—but what he likes most is being himself. The Bully: He has shades of this. He makes fun of Alabaster a lot. Distaff Counterpart: He's fun-loving like Topaz. Fun Personified: He likes parties and music. Of the boys he's the life of the party. Hair Today, Gone Tomorrow: Jasper is seen with an afro at first, then Amethyst's spell makes him lose his hair. He decided it looked cool and keeps it shaved.

characters.name: Flint
characters.description: Onyx's boyfriend. Flint is an emo poet who plays a rocking guitar, and while his poems may be out-there, his heart is in the right place. Birds of a Feather: With Onyx. They're aGothcouple. Distaff Counterpart: He's a goth like Onyx. The Heart: Of the boys. He's the most in tune with his emotions, and gives the best relationship advice. Mellow Fellow: He rarely gets upset about anything. The most reaction he has is making up a song about what's happening.

characters.name: Coral, Opal, and Jade
characters.description: A trio of cheerleaders at Trollzopolis High School who're frequently mean to the BFFL. Coral in particular is spoiled, haughty, and perpetually stuck-up. Alpha Bitch: All three are this, but Coral's definitely the biggest one. Cruel Cheerleader: All three are mean-spirited, stuck-up cheerleaders, with Coral even being the head cheerleader for Trollzopolis High. Evil Laugh: Coral has a laugh that sounds like a witch's cackle. Fiery Redhead: Coral's hair is red and brown and she's quite bitchy.

characters.name: Mica and Shale
characters.description: Villains for episodes 7-9. Mica is a hotshot Skoot racer, while Shale is his girlfriend, and they have designs on Sapphire to help Mica win the big race. Arc Villain: Villains of The Big Race arc. Exotic Eye Designs: Subverted; Shale wears contacts. Heel–Face Turn: Mica turns good in episode 9, after the girls save his life. Jerk with a Heart of Gold: They're both jerks, but Shale admits that Mica should have been thankful for Onyx saving his life and Mica is thankful for the girls saving his life in the next episode.

characters.name: Kaboom
characters.description: The test subject for many of the Spell Moments, whether he likes it or not. The Dog Bites Back: The spell moment in "Not-So-Good Old Days" features Ruby using a spell to make Kaboom's ears grow abnormally large. Kaboom uses a callerIDthat makes the spell afffect Ruby's ears as well. Iron Butt-Monkey: Each episode has a "spell moment" where one of the BFFL uses Kaboom as a test subject for a spell andHilarity Ensues. No matter what happens to him on each spell moment, his next appearance shows no residual effects.
